en.ascii: JoeRunner, T.T., and Harry play an online game.

en.html: A computer screen shows a video game, Hyper Galactic Battle Ducks III. The player is tomatotoe07, and the time is 0757. The player’s ship is shaped like a mouse cursor. They have a middling Physical bar, and a low Energy bar. There is a chat in the bottom left corner.

brightful On the move!

brightful Uh-oh!

sharkles5 stop spamming defaults

brightful I’m using tilt controls!
(off-panel) Ha! I berzork’d your rear energy shields!

en.ascii: JoeRunner and T.T. are sitting next to each other at computers. JoeRunner looks very confident.

en.text: . . . but that got me enough reputation to access the Pan‐Galactic Gargle Blaster from the Retnia shop!

en.ascii: JoeRunner is imagining his ship blowing up another, while his duck avatar gives a thumbs up.

en.text: JoeRunner raises his eyebrows, and a burst symbol appears above his head. It turns into a loading wheel with the text “Processing . . .” as JoeRunner stares blankly at the screen.

en.html: (looking over, raising eyebrow) What, what is it?

en.ascii: T.T. raises his hand and gets an annoyed look.

en.html: Every day there are what, like 300 players and he keeps finding us?

en.text: I swear he’s just trolling us at this point.

en.ascii: A boy on another computer with long black hair and an unbuttoned polo shirt has a smug look on his face. He speaks in a textured font.
Ah, guilty as charged. It is just so funny when you guys rage.

en.text: Harry / gamer of the highest order

en.html: (surprised) Harry, you’ve been ottohammr this whole time?

en.ascii: The last four years, anyway. And now you are approximately 1.39729 * 10^12 molecules of space debris.
en.text: The last four years, anyway. And now you are approximately 1.39729 × 10¹² molecules of space debris.
en.html: The last four years, anyway. And now you are approximately 1.39729 × 10<sup>12</sup> molecules of space debris.

en.ascii: T.T. and Harry dap each other up.
But, my friends, it was quite an honorable battle.

en.html:
Alas, poor tomatotoe07.

This page only took a month to come out instead of, like, five. There’s so much that I want to get to, and it’s a bit upsetting to me that I’ve only gotten this far, with JoeRunner and Co.’s first anniversary being in two days (April 1). This page especially took a lot of effort⁠—the most yet⁠—due to its length.

Only about 60% of the draft I wrote back on . . . checks May 25, 2021? I really am behind. ahem Only about 60% of the original draft is in this final version. None of these pages so far are super faithful to that draft⁠—I wrote them when I was around 14 years old, and of course my dialogue has changed a lot in two years of my teenage life. It means the same thing, but it’s expanded and, dare I say, funnier. Assuming it’s funny at all.

I have 30 more pages drafted out, and while undoubtedly not all of it will stay, I’m excited to get through them. I’ve decided that I can get a comic done every week, posted on Thursday, while still doing school. So I’ll try it, and we’ll see how it goes.

Once again, I so sincerely appreciate everyone who reads my silly, medium–low quality webcomic. I hope that you’ve enjoyed them regardless.

—⁠ ⁠Gabriel / gabl
